T0.sql:
SELECT COALESCE(p.column_name, u.column_name) AS column_name,
                   p.ordinal_position, u.ordinal_position, p.data_type, u.data_type
            FROM (SELECT * FROM PROD_DB.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
                  WHERE table_schema = 'BI' AND table_name = 'LEARNING_ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_QUIZ_SUBMISSION') p
            FULL JOIN (SELECT * FROM UAT_DB.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
                  WHERE table_schema = 'BI' AND table_name = 'LEARNING_ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_QUIZ_SUBMISSION') u
              ON p.column_name = u.column_name
            WHERE p.column_name IS NULL OR u.column_name IS NULL
               OR p.ordinal_position != u.ordinal_position
               OR p.data_type != u.data_type
            ORDER BY COALESCE(p.ordinal_position, u.ordinal_position)

T1.sql:
SELECT p.n, u.n, p.n - u.n
            FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM PROD_DB.BI.LEARNING_ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_QUIZ_SUBMISSION t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region) p,
                 (SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM UAT_DB.BI.LEARNING_ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_QUIZ_SUBMISSION t  INNER JOIN UAT_DB.BI.TEMP_ACTIVE_TENANTS AS act ON t.client_name = act.client_name AND t.client_region = act.client_region) u

EMPLOYEES - Confirming your understanding and ensuring all induction tasks have been covered/familiarised is an essential HSE requirement.  When answering the questions please ensure you add sufficient details and information so that managers can be confident of your understanding when marking and completing the induction checklists.
MANAGERS - please review all employee responses, before signing off the induction you need to ensure the answers provide you with sufficient detail for you (or any other manager) to be 100% confident the employee has been familiarised and fully understands the requirements of each induction task checkpoint.  
'Yes/No' responses are not sufficient for open questions or questions that require sufficient details for you to be confident of the employees understanding prior to signing off the induction.
If an employee has answered NO to any of the induction tasks you will need to re-train/familiarise them with the task & requirements, and then mark the checklist <100% & add feedback on what's required to pass the checklist & submit - this will fail the marking and reject it back to the employee so they can edit it and resubmit.
If you are happy with all induction task responses, and fully confident the employee is competent and understands all areas and tasks covered in the induction then mark it at 100%, add your feedback, then submit to pass this part of the induction.

Hi Judy.
Thanks for submitting your Products and Services quiz. You’ve provided some great answers and gone into a good level of detail. Please could you check over my feedback comments below, update your answers and resubmit your quiz. Feel free to contact me directly if you have any queries:
QUESTION 1 - Good info you've provided here. Just one small query - you said the child can get a redicard at age 13, but he can get one earlier than that (it's a VISA card at 13). Please could you update your answer to have the correct date for a redicard. The BU Savings Account policy might help here.
QUESTSION 1, 3 and 10 - Finally, I like how you added the benefits to question 2, but you've only got features listed for questions 1, 3 and 10. For each feature that you have provided please also write down the corresponding benefit to the member of that feature. Remember the feature is the fact about the account, and the benefit is why that fact is a good thing for our customers. For example, Feature: “Internet and mobile banking”. Benefit: “Members can easily access their funds on the move, 24/7”. Look at the Product Activity in the Practice Activity Workbook for more ideas or to refresh your memory.
Let me know if you aren’t sure what I mean at all!
Thanks!

I would recommend the Purple Bonus Saver Account because as he is putting regular payments in, he satisfies the 'over $50' requirement this account has. Because this is a long term goal, he can refrain from withdrawing any funds so that the account will gain the bonus interest of 0.40% pa - which is calculated daily and paid monthly. Internet and mobile banking access is available for this account so he can keep a track of the balance as it grows at a moment's notice - where ever he may be.

Diabetes: Impaired circulation, poor wound healing, and nerve damage can increase the risk of pressure injuries in individuals with diabetes.Peripheral Vascular Disease (PVD): Reduced blood flow to the skin can make it harder for the body to repair damaged tissue, making individuals with PVD more susceptible to pressure injuries.Malnutrition: Inadequate nutrition weakens the skin, reduces its ability to heal, and increases the risk of tissue breakdown.Obesity: Excess weight can increase pressure on certain areas of the body, especially when mobility is limited, leading to a higher risk of pressure injury formation.Immobility or Limited Mobility: Conditions such as stroke, spinal cord injury, or neurological disorders can impair movement, increasing the likelihood of prolonged pressure on skin, which leads to pressure injuries.Incontinence: Urinary or fecal incontinence can cause moisture-related damage to the skin, increasing the risk of breakdown, especially when combined with pressure.Chronic Respiratory Conditions: Conditions like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) can limit mobility and contribute to hypoxia (lack of oxygen), which impairs tissue health and healing.Age: Older adults often have thinner, less resilient skin, reduced subcutaneous fat, and poorer circulation, which can make them more vulnerable to pressure injuries.Cancer: Certain cancers or cancer treatments (such as chemotherapy or radiation) can impair skin integrity and healing, increasing the risk of pressure injuries.Cognitive Impairment: Conditions like dementia or Alzheimer's can limit a person’s ability to understand the need for repositioning or communicate discomfort, contributing to the development of pressure injuries.
Name the co-morbidity factors can be contributed pressure injury

Refresher assessment completed with Fiti on Knife Handling. Using the the SOP as the guide. Fiti was well prepared for his assessment. Fiti was dressed correctly for his Knife handling refresher assessment, dressed in all the correct PPE equipment rquired for the task, including having the 2 knives, which the SOP allows. Observing Fiti trimming for L3, Fiti was trimming excess fat from the Pork loin steaks that were given back to him from the packers as they were grading out. Fiti kept a good safe distance from the packers allowing both himself and the packer plenty of room, Fiti's knife technique was comfortable and he showed no signs of discomfort. His body positioning was good ensuring both knives were pouched when not in use. Fiti kept a good tidy work area and the Red bin was in close proximity where he could easily dispose of waste, after approximately 15mins I mentioned to Fiti that I was happy with what I had observed. - Assessed Competent

Does the operator stand in a safe proximity from the next worker, 1.5 meters apart and not across from each other?

The safe proximity distance between fellow knife hands should be 1.5 meters, and not directly across from each other.
Very competent should use this more often

Attention Employee - You cannot complete the VOC unsupervised. Speak to your Manager on how to complete the VOC.Attention Assessor - Your role is to physically assess the employee that this VOC is allocated to. This must be completed with the applicable equipment on site. Once this is submitted the VOC will be emailed to the Marking Manager to finalise the process (Accept or Reject the VOC completion).Attention - Marking Manager - When marking and approving the VOC, you are indicating whether the VOC content is or isn't acceptable. Additionally, you will also need to ensure that you confirm with the assessor (if you were not the one assessing) that the VOC was conducted under supervision. Note - The Assessor and the Marking Manager may be the same person.You will need to provide feedback in the feedback box.Example - I, 'John Doe', agree that the the VOC content is adequate and that all checks have been made to ensure the VOC was completed correctly under supervision.

Paper Mill - Reel to Winder Quiz
General Safety Awareness Paper Mill - Reel to Winder Quiz

machine guards are there for a reason it is to protect yourself and others working in and around the area, machine guards usually are places in certain areas of the machinery that are dangerous. if accessed beyond machine guards could potentially cause harm or serious injury. on the winder there is a general emergency stoop button and there is also a crawl emergency stop which is a foot operated bar switch. before lowering the cradle to the floor making sure yourself or no one else is standing in the black and yellow marker floor area, checking that the area is clear Infront of lowering cradle before pressing the winder cradle roll release, when the cradle is lowering the cradle lowering audible alarm will sound to make sure everyone is aware that it is lowering. Upton discovering a fire in the dryers, the following needs to be done to minimize damage and risk to people and the plant. break sheet back to center roll using break jet buttons on each control desk. when active fire in dryers press fire switch if on pm2 switch is located on the 2nd, 3rd, 4th dryer section. if on pm3 switch located on the control panel, 3rd, 4th dryer section control panel this will interlock certain drives. active deluge system , pm2 active by opening 1 of 2 ball valve controllers. located southern side of stainless panel next to 2nd dryer panel. located at the entrance to basement doors. pm3 active by opening 1 of 3 ball valve controllers. located north side of 2a dryer section panel. lower hood doors do not stop any felts or dryers call 380 to activate the ERT the emergency alarm will sound and a red beacon will flash for evacuation.
MACHINE COMPONENTS COULD ROTATE OR SHIFT POSITION AS PART OF AN AUTOMATED CHANGE SEQUENCE.

T4.sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS shared_keys,
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_ID", u."ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_ID")) AS "ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_ID",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Marked By User",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Marked By User",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Marked By User",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Quiz Submission Score", u."Quiz Submission Score")) AS "Quiz Submission Score",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Feedback",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Feedback",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Feedback",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Quiz Submission Submitted Date", u."Quiz Submission Submitted Date")) AS "Quiz Submission Submitted Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(p."Quiz Submission Marked Date", u."Quiz Submission Marked Date")) AS "Quiz Submission Marked Date",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Status",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Status",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Status",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Answer",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Answer",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Answer",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Question",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Question",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Question",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Section",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Section",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Section",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Name",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Name",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Name",
       COUNT_IF(NOT EQUAL_NULL(NULLIF(p."Quiz Submission Introduction",''), NULLIF(u."Quiz Submission Introduction",''))) AS "Quiz Submission Introduction"
FROM PROD_DB.BI.LEARNING_ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_QUIZ_SUBMISSION p
JOIN UAT_DB.BI.LEARNING_ENROLMENT_ACTIVITY_QUIZ_SUBMISSION u USING ("Quiz Submission ID", "QUESTION_ID", "CLIENT_NAME", "CLIENT_REGION")
